attractions.name: 1889 Farms (Roy'zzz Premium Cannabis)
attractions.description: 1889 Farms is a premium cannabis brand featured at Roy'zzz Premium Cannabis in Sioux Falls, South Dakota. The name pays homage to 1889, the year South Dakota achieved statehood, and the shop offers a wide selection of high-quality cannabis products in a modern retail setting.
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat: 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM, Sun: 2:00 PM - 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Retail store entry)

attractions.tips: Customers must be 21 years of age or older and present a valid government-issued photo ID to enter and make purchases.
attractions.nearby: The Empire Mall, Western Mall, and numerous restaurants on 41st Street.

attractions.name: 63rd Annual Sidewalk Arts Festival
attractions.description: The Sidewalk Arts Festival is the region’s largest one-day arts festival and a favorite annual tradition in downtown Sioux Falls. It features more than 230 vendors showcasing fine art, folk art, and handcrafted goods from across the region, along with food vendors and live entertainment. (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.hours: Saturday: 9 am - 5 pm (Annual one-day event) (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.admission: Free (Washington Pavilion)
highlights: 'Over 230 fine art, folk art, and craft vendors ([Washington Pavilion)', 'Live entertainment and performances throughout the day (Washington Pavilion)', 'Interactive activities designed for kids and families (Washington Pavilion)', 'Wide selection of local food vendors and downtown dining options (Washington Pavilion)', 'Fundraiser supporting the Washington Pavilion of Arts & Science (Washington Pavilion)']
attractions.tips: The festival is a smoke-free event; ADA accommodations are available by contacting the Washington Pavilion at least 72 hours in advance. (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.nearby: Washington Pavilion of Arts & Science, SculptureWalk Sioux Falls, Falls Park, Levitt at the Falls (Experience Sioux Falls)

attractions.name: Arc of Dreams
attractions.description: The Arc of Dreams is a monumental stainless-steel sculpture spanning the Big Sioux River in downtown Sioux Falls, stretching nearly the length of a football field. Created by South Dakota Artist Laureate Dale Claude Lamphere, the landmark features a signature 15-foot gap at its center, suspended 85 feet above the water to symbolize the 'leap of faith' taken by dreamers (SculptureWalk Sioux Falls, Experience Sioux Falls).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Atlas Obscura)
attractions.admission: Free (Experience Sioux Falls, Atlas Obscura)
highlights: 'Stretches nearly 300 feet across the Big Sioux River ([Dale Lamphere Studio)', "Features a 15-foot gap representing a dreamer's 'leap of faith' (SculptureWalk Sioux Falls)", 'Rises up to 85 feet above the river surface (Experience Sioux Falls)', 'Constructed from massive stainless-steel telescoping pipes (Albertson Engineering)', 'Illuminated at night with customizable LED lighting displays (Atlas Obscura)', 'Serves as the capstone of the Sioux Falls SculptureWalk (Experience Sioux Falls)']
attractions.tips: The sculpture is best viewed at night when it is beautifully illuminated with colored lights. It is fully accessible with no steps, making it easy for wheelchairs and strollers to reach via the nearby bike trails and river greenway (Airial Travel, SculptureWalk Sioux Falls).
attractions.nearby: Falls Park, 8th Street Arch Bridge, Downtown Sioux Falls, Washington Pavilion, SculptureWalk (Experience Sioux Falls, Vern Eide Rentals)

attractions.name: Augustana University
attractions.description: Augustana University is a private Lutheran university located in the heart of Sioux Falls, South Dakota, founded in 1860. As the largest private university in the state, it serves over 2,500 students and is recognized for its strong liberal arts curriculum and vibrant campus life (Augustana University, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 2001 S. Summit Ave., Sioux Falls, SD 57197 (Augustana University)
attractions.hours: Administrative/Visit Hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m.; Center for Western Studies: Mon-Fri 8:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Eide/Dalrymple Gallery: Mon-Fri 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m., Sat 1:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m. (Augustana University, Experience Sioux Falls)
attractions.admission: Free for campus tours and museum/gallery visits; athletic and theatre events require separate tickets (Augustana University, Experience Sioux Falls)
highlights: ['Ole the Viking Statue, a campus landmark designed by alumnus Peter Eide', 'Center for Western Studies, featuring a museum, library, and archives dedicated to the Northern Plains', 'Eide/Dalrymple Gallery, showcasing professional and student art exhibitions throughout the year', 'Kirkeby-Over Stadium, a premier 6,500-seat football venue on campus', 'Froiland Science Complex, a state-of-the-art facility for research and learning', "Mikkelsen Library, the university's main research and study hub"]
attractions.tips: Visitor parking is available in the lot on Summit Avenue, two blocks north of 33rd Street. Visiting during "Viking Days" in the fall offers a chance to experience the university's largest homecoming traditions. Don't miss the chance to see the "Ole" statue in the center of the quad, which is often decorated for seasonal events (Augustana University, Augustana University).
attractions.nearby: McKennan Park, Sanford Pentagon, Midco Arena, Great Plains Zoo, Falls Park (Experience Sioux Falls, Augustana University)

attractions.name: Avera McKennan Healing Gardens (Prairie Center)
attractions.description: The Avera McKennan campus features multiple serene spaces, most notably the indoor and outdoor healing gardens at the Avera Cancer Institute - Prairie Center. These gardens include native South Dakota plantings, flowing waterfalls, and a meditation labyrinth designed to provide a restorative environment for patients, families, and visitors.

attractions.hours: Campus: 24/7; Prairie Center Lobby: Monday – Friday: 8 AM – 5 PM (Avera Health)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Hospital campus); Weekdays (Prairie Center lobby) (Avera Health)
attractions.admission: Free (Avera Health)
highlights: 'Indoor healing gardens with 7,500 sq. ft. of plants and trees [BWBR', 'Outdoor meditation labyrinth with stones from the Crazy Horse Memorial BWBR', 'Multi-story indoor waterfalls and tranquil ponds Schuler Shook', 'Rooftop garden and patio with expansive city views Avera Health', 'Heritage Wall in the lobby reflecting the mission of the founding Sisters SiouxFalls.Business', 'Indoor SculptureWalk featuring regional art collections Avera Health']
attractions.tips: Visit the Prairie Center during weekday business hours to fully access the indoor garden and atrium. Free valet parking is available at the Prairie Center entrance. Check out the SculptureWalk located throughout the hospital corridors for additional art and serenity.
attractions.nearby: McKennan Park (Sunken Gardens, Rose Garden, Bandshell), Avera McKennan Hospital main campus, Café 1325 (City of Sioux Falls)

attractions.name: Big Sioux Recreation Area
attractions.description: Big Sioux Recreation Area lies on the banks of the Big Sioux River and offers a diverse landscape of prairie, forest, and wetlands. It is a popular destination for camping, hiking, biking, and river-based activities, providing a natural escape just minutes from the city. South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM South Dakota GFP Policies
attractions.admission: Daily: $10 for residents, $15 for nonresidents; Annual: $40 for residents, $60 for nonresidents. Campsites and cabins have additional fees. South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
attractions.phone: (605) 582-7243 (Local Office) / (605) 773-8106 (State Office) Big Sioux Recreation Area Rules
highlights: ['18-hole disc golf course with equipment available for checkout', 'Archery range and archery trails on the west side of the river', 'Miles of hiking and biking trails, including the scenic Trail of the Giants loop', 'Canoeing and kayaking access to the Big Sioux River', 'Winter sports including snowmobiling, cross-country skiing, and a warming house', 'Historical interest sites including an old cabin on the grounds']
attractions.tips: Equipment such as fishing poles, disc golf discs, and snowshoes can be checked out from the park office. For the best birdwatching, visit in mid-May to see migrating warblers. Be aware that showers and water systems are typically closed from October 1 to April 30. South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks, South Dakota Birding
attractions.nearby: Great Bear Recreation Park, Mary Jo Wegner Arboretum, Arrowhead Park, Falls Park, Good Earth State Park, Palisades State Park Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Big Sioux River Recreation Trail and Greenway
attractions.description: The Big Sioux River Recreation Trail and Greenway is an extensive network of over 45 miles of paved trails that loop around the city of Sioux Falls. Following the scenic Big Sioux River, the system connects numerous city parks and offers a diverse landscape of waterfalls, open fields, and forested areas for year-round outdoor activities like biking and walking (City of Sioux Falls).
attractions.address: 231 N. Dakota Ave (Main Office), 131 E. Falls Park Dr (Primary Access Point)
attractions.region: City-wide Loop / Downtown (City of Sioux Falls)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 5:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Falls Park and Downtown sections open until Midnight) (City of Sioux Falls)
attractions.admission: Free (Experience Sioux Falls)
highlights: '19-mile main trail loop circling the city ([City of Sioux Falls)', 'Direct access to the iconic Falls Park and its waterfalls (City of Sioux Falls)', 'Connections to more than 11 major city parks and access points (City of Sioux Falls)', 'Designated paths for biking, walking, running, and rollerblading (City of Sioux Falls)', 'Equipped with convenient restrooms and bicycle fix-it stations (City of Sioux Falls)']
attractions.tips: Observe the 15 mph speed limit on all trail sections, stay to the right except when passing, and provide an audible signal before overtaking others. For safety, helmets are strongly encouraged, and users should check the interactive map for any active trail detours or closures (City of Sioux Falls).
attractions.nearby: Falls Park, Downtown Sioux Falls, Fawick Park, Spencer Park, Great Bear Ski Valley (Experience Sioux Falls)

attractions.name: Brioux Falls Coffee
attractions.description: Brioux Falls is an urban grab-and-go coffee destination nestled in the heart of The Steel District in downtown Sioux Falls. It offers premium handcrafted coffee sourced from Driven Coffee Roasters alongside a variety of gourmet breakfast and lunch options.
attractions.hours: Mon–Fri: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Sat: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sun: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Brioux Falls Coffee)
attractions.admission: Free (Brioux Falls Coffee)
highlights: 'Sourced from Driven Coffee Roasters, an artisan roaster in the Twin Cities ([Brioux Falls Coffee)', 'Located on the second floor of the nine-story Steel District office tower with skywalk access (Brioux Falls Coffee)', 'Features a menu of Lotus energy drinks, Rishi Teas, smoothies, and protein shakes (Brioux Falls Coffee)', 'Offers gourmet grab-and-go food such as maple waffle bacon sandwiches and charcuterie boards (The Steel District)', 'Modern, sleek interior with expansive windows providing views of the evolving district (Downtown Sioux Falls)', 'Conveniently located near the Big Sioux River and Falls Park (Travel South Dakota)']
attractions.tips: The shop is located on the 2nd floor of the office tower, accessible via the skywalk from the Steel District Parking Ramp. Parking in the ramp is free for the first 15 minutes during the week and free all day on weekends and after 5 PM on weekdays. (Brioux Falls Coffee Directions)
attractions.nearby: Falls Park, Big Sioux River, Levitt at the Falls, The Steel District retail area, Downtown Sioux Falls (Phillips Avenue) (Experience Sioux Falls)

attractions.name: Butterfly House & Aquarium
attractions.description: The Butterfly House & Aquarium is a year-round tropical destination in Sioux Falls that combines a lush butterfly conservatory with South Dakota's only public saltwater aquarium. It is a nonprofit organization dedicated to inspiring conservation through hands-on educational experiences with butterflies, corals, and marine life (Great Plains Zoo).
attractions.address: 4320 S Oxbow Ave, Sioux Falls, SD 57106
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 10 am - 4 pm (Final admission at 3:30 pm) (Great Plains Zoo)
attractions.days_open: Open year-round, seven days a week (closed on New Year's Day, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve Day, and Christmas Day) (Great Plains Zoo)
attractions.admission: Infant (Under age 2): Free, Youth (Ages 2-17): $11 + Tax, Adult (Ages 18-59): $15 + Tax, Senior (Ages 60+): $13 + Tax (Great Plains Zoo)
highlights: 'Over 800 free-flying butterflies from around the world in a tropical conservatory ([Experience Sioux Falls)', 'The only public saltwater aquarium in the Dakotas featuring marine fishes, jellyfish, and corals (Great Plains Zoo)', 'Interactive Pacific Tide Pool where visitors can touch starfish and sea cucumbers (Tripadvisor)', 'Touch tank with the only stingray and shark encounter in a 600-mile radius (Just Short of Crazy)', 'Year-round tropical environment kept at a constant 80 degrees (Just Short of Crazy)', 'Fins & Flutters Gift Shop for unique nature-themed gifts and educational toys (Experience Sioux Falls)']
attractions.tips: Wear bright colors like red or orange to attract butterflies to land on you. Use the outdoor stroller parking as space inside is limited. Consider buying tickets online to skip the lines. Be mindful of where you step in the flight room as butterflies may land on walkways (Just Short of Crazy, Great Plains Zoo).

attractions.name: Cathedral of Saint Joseph Official Website
attractions.description: The Cathedral of Saint Joseph is a majestic Romanesque and French Renaissance style cathedral that serves as a beacon of hope for the Diocese of Sioux Falls. Completed in 1919 and designed by renowned architect Emmanuel Masqueray, the building features iconic twin 185-foot spires that dominate the city's skyline. Official Website, American Planning Association

attractions.region: Cathedral Historic District (historically known as Nob Hill) Wikipedia), American Planning Association

attractions.name: Central Fire Station (Station No. 1)
attractions.description: Central Fire Station, built in 1913, is one of the oldest working fire stations west of the Mississippi and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. The station serves as a functional firehouse while also housing a collection of the department's historical artifacts and restored antique apparatuses. It features a unique design originally built to accommodate horse-drawn equipment and includes displays of a 1880 Silsby Steamer and a 1915 Seagrave pumper. City of Sioux Falls, Wikipedia), SAH Archipedia
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 am to 5:00 pm City of Sioux Falls
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday City of Sioux Falls
attractions.admission: Free City of Sioux Falls, Stephen Travels

Wikipedia)', 'Houses the restored 1880 Silsby Steamer and hand pumper. City of Sioux Falls', "Displays the 1915 Seagrave pumper, the city's first motorized fire truck. City of Sioux Falls", "Contains historical log books, handwritten reports, and photos of the department's history. City of Sioux Falls", 'Features classic quartzite and limestone architecture representative of early 20th-century Sioux Falls. TSP, Inc.']
attractions.tips: The historical displays, including the 1880 Silsby Steamer, are located inside the main level of the station and are generally viewable during office hours. Note that this is an active, working fire station, so be mindful of personnel and apparatus movements. City of Sioux Falls, Facebook

attractions.name: Delbridge Museum of Natural History
attractions.description: The Delbridge Museum of Natural History featured one of the Midwest's most comprehensive collections of taxidermy, containing over 150 mounts from six continents. The collection, known as the Brockhouse Collection, was donated to the City of Sioux Falls in 1981 and was a staple of the Great Plains Zoo for nearly 40 years until its permanent closure in August 2023 due to arsenic concerns in the specimens (Great Plains Zoo, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 805 S Kiwanis Ave, Sioux Falls, SD 57104
attractions.hours: Historically 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Summer), 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Winter) (Great Plains Zoo).
attractions.days_open: Historically Open Daily (WhichMuseum). Currently Closed (Great Plains Zoo).
attractions.admission: Historically included with Great Plains Zoo admission: Adults (13-59): $18.95; Youth (2-12): $14.95; Seniors (60+), Military, and Students: $17.95; Infants (under 2): Free (Great Plains Zoo).
highlights: 'Brockhouse Collection of over 150 taxidermy mounts ([Great Plains Zoo)', 'Display of 38 "vanishing species" (Wikipedia)', 'Large-scale dioramas representing natural habitats from six continents (The New York Times)', 'Significant specimens including an African elephant, polar bear, and walrus (Pigeon605)']
attractions.tips: The museum is permanently closed to the public as of August 2023. The collection is currently being deaccessioned, with many specimens being gifted to institutions like the University of Notre Dame for scientific and educational purposes (Great Plains Zoo, Independent).

attractions.name: Denny Sanford PREMIER Center
attractions.description: The Denny Sanford PREMIER Center is a multi-purpose indoor arena located in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, serving as the region's premier destination for large-scale concerts and sporting events. Opened in 2014, it boasts a capacity of up to 13,000 spectators and is seamlessly connected to the Sioux Falls Arena and Convention Center. The facility is home to the Sioux Falls Stampede hockey team and hosts a diverse array of national touring acts, family shows, and championship tournaments. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Phone Only); Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Phone Only); Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Phone Only); Saturday & Sunday: Closed (Open at 12:00 PM on Event Days). (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Event Days for in-person box office; Monday-Friday for phone inquiries. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; tickets typically range from ~$40 to several hundred dollars depending on seating tier and performance type. Tickets are sold via Ticketmaster and AXS. (Official Website, Event Tickets Center)
highlights: 'Largest indoor venue in the state of South Dakota ([Wikipedia)', 'Home to the Sioux Falls Stampede hockey team (USHL) (Wikipedia)', "Annual host of the Summit League Men's and Women's Basketball Championships (Wikipedia)", 'Integrated complex connected to the Sioux Falls Arena and Convention Center (Experience Sioux Falls)', 'Certified Sensory Inclusive venue with dedicated resources for guests (Official Website)', 'Host to major national tours including Paul McCartney, Elton John, and Garth Brooks (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The venue enforces a clear bag policy (max 12"x12"x6"). Visit the box office in person on Tuesdays or Thursdays to save on some online ticketing fees. On-site parking is generally free in the surrounding lots. The entire facility is non-smoking, but designated outdoor smoking areas are provided. (Official Website, Experience Sioux Falls)
attractions.nearby: Falls Park, Sioux Empire Fairgrounds, Washington Pavilion of Arts and Science, Sioux Falls Arena, Sioux Falls Convention Center. (Expedia, Wikipedia)

Centered around the iconic 8th & Railroad Center, the area features beautifully repurposed industrial buildings that house everything from art galleries and boutiques to breweries and specialty markets. It serves as a cultural hub where history meets modern urban living, offering a pedestrian-friendly environment along the Big Sioux River.

attractions.description: Tuthill Park is a scenic 80-acre green space in southern Sioux Falls, renowned for its panoramic views of the Big Sioux River bluffs and its historic Tuthill House and Garden (City of Sioux Falls). attractions.address: 3500 S. Cliff Ave. (City of Sioux Falls)
attractions.region: Tuthill Park Neighborhood (City of Sioux Falls)
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 05:00 AM – 10:00 PM (City of Sioux Falls)
attractions.admission: Free (Falls Area Singletrack, City of Sioux Falls)
highlights: 'Challenging 18-hole disc golf course regarded as one of the best in the region ([Kupi.com).', 'Historic Tuthill House and Garden, featuring a formal garden and gazebo for weddings (City of Sioux Falls).', 'Four miles of machine-built singletrack trails for mountain biking, running, and hiking (Falls Area Singletrack).', 'Extensive winter recreation area with a large sledding hill and an outdoor ice rink with a warming house (Experience Sioux Falls).', 'Scenic overlooks providing breathtaking views of the Big Sioux River and city skyline (Kupi.com).', 'Direct connection to the Big Sioux River Recreation Trail and Greenway system (Experience Sioux Falls).']
attractions.tips: Alcohol is strictly prohibited throughout the park by city ordinance (City of Sioux Falls). If planning a wedding or event, reservations must be made at least five business days in advance and no more than 120 days out (City of Sioux Falls). For disc golf enthusiasts, scorecards and aerial maps are conveniently located at the top of the hill (Experience Sioux Falls).

The 40-acre main campus features a mix of historic buildings and modern facilities, serving as a central hub for higher education in the city. University of Sioux Falls, Wikipedia

attractions.name: USS South Dakota Battleship Memorial
attractions.description: The USS South Dakota Battleship Memorial is a unique tribute to the most decorated battleship of World War II, featuring a full-scale outline of the ship's main deck on its grounds. The memorial's mission is to preserve the history of the USS South Dakota (BB-57) and its crew through a museum filled with artifacts, original ship pieces, and educational displays. (Official Site, Experience Sioux Falls)
attractions.hours: Daily 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Seasonal: Open May 1st to September 30th) (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: 7 Days a Week (May 1st to September 30th) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations welcomed) (whichmuseum.com, Tripadvisor)
highlights: "A 680-foot concrete and lawn outline representing the ship's actual size ([Lake.com)", 'Original artifacts including a 16-inch gun, anchor, propeller, and radar equipment (Exploring States)', 'The on-site museum containing crew memorabilia and historical photos (Official Site)', 'An eternal flame dedicated to the memory of the ship and its crew (Official Site)', "Educational exhibits detailing the ship's service in every major Pacific naval battle from 1942-1945 (Experience Sioux Falls)", 'Statue of a sailor looking out to sea (Exploring States)']
attractions.tips: The memorial is seasonal and closed from October 1st to April 30th, though the grounds may remain accessible. Plan to spend 1-2 hours exploring both the outdoor ship outline and the indoor museum. Wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the large riverside grounds. (Tripadvisor, Lake.com)

attractions.name: Washington High School Auditorium
attractions.description: The Washington High School Auditorium is a 900-seat performance venue that serves as the heart of the school's fine arts and community programs. Recently renovated to modernize its facilities, it hosts a wide range of events from Broadway-style show choir competitions to theatrical productions and student pageants.
attractions.hours: Office: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Mon-Fri); Events: Typically 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM on weeknights or all-day during weekend competitions.
attractions.admission: Event-specific: Typically $15 for all-day competitions, $10 for students and senior citizens, and $5 for specific theater performances or pageants. Free for certain school-day events.
highlights: ['900-seat capacity designed for optimal acoustics and visibility.', "Host venue for the annual 'Best of Show' show choir competition.", 'Recently renovated to provide state-of-the-art theater and music facilities.', "Home to the Washington High School Theatre Department's seasonal productions.", "Primary site for the 'Mr. WHS' Pageant and other student-led cultural events.", 'Equipped with professional-grade sound and lighting systems for varied performances.']
attractions.tips: Visitors should enter through the 'T' Doors (Faculty Doors) on the west side of the building near the tennis courts. Parking is most accessible in the west lot, and tickets for many events are sold as cash-only at the door.

attractions.name: Washington Pavilion of Arts and Science (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.description: The Washington Pavilion is the region’s premier destination for arts, entertainment, and science, located in a beautifully renovated historic school building in downtown Sioux Falls. It uniquely houses the Kirby Science Discovery Center, the Visual Arts Center, and the Husby Performing Arts Center under one roof, providing a multifaceted cultural experience for visitors of all ages (Washington Pavilion).
attractions.hours: Museums & Box Office: Mon–Sat 10 a.m.–5 p.m., Sun 12–5 p.m.; WP Café: Mon–Fri 10 a.m.–2 p.m., Sat 10 a.m.–3 p.m., Sun 12–3 p.m.; Parlour: Mon–Sat 11 a.m.–5 p.m., Sun 12–5 p.m. (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.days_open: Monday–Sunday (Closed on New Year’s Day, Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day) (Washington Pavilion)
attractions.admission: Adult (18+): $16.25; Student/Youth (3–17 or with student ID): $13.50; Seniors (65+) and Military: $14.00; Children 2 and under: Free; Members: Free. Discounts include $1.00 off online bookings, 50% off for the Afternoon Special (starts at 3 p.m.), and Free First Fridays from 5–8 p.m. (Washington Pavilion)
highlights: 'Kirby Science Discovery Center with over 100 interactive exhibits ([Experience Sioux Falls)', 'Visual Arts Center featuring eight unique galleries of changing exhibits (Washington Pavilion)', 'Husby Performing Arts Center, home to Broadway productions and symphonic concerts (Experience Sioux Falls)', 'Wells Fargo CineDome & Sweetman Planetarium for immersive films and stargazing (Washington Pavilion)', 'The WP Café and Parlour Ice Cream for on-site dining and treats (Washington Pavilion)']

It is dedicated to preserving and interpreting the cultural and historical heritage of Lake County and the eastern South Dakota region from the mid-1800s to the present. attractions.name: Newton Hills State Park
attractions.description: Newton Hills State Park is a 1,063-acre forested oasis nestled within the rolling hills of the Coteau des Prairie geological formation. Created by glacial movement, the park features a unique dark forest amidst the open prairie and has been a site of human habitation since 300 B.C., with legends of buried gold and robbers' hideouts adding to its charm. Wikipedia, South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
attractions.hours: Park Hours: 6 a.m. – 11 p.m. daily. Visitor Center Hours: Summer (June–Sept) 8 a.m. – 4 p.m. daily (open until 10 p.m. Fri–Sat); Winter (Oct–May) 8 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Mon–Fri. Travel South Dakota
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday–Sunday) South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
attractions.admission: Daily: $10 for residents, $15 for nonresidents; Annual: $40 for residents, $60 for nonresidents. Campsites, cabins, and group lodges are available at varying nightly rates ($16–$280). South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
highlights: ['Over 5 miles of interconnected hiking and mountain biking trails', 'Lake Lakota for fishing, swimming, kayaking, and paddleboarding', 'Observation tower offering panoramic views over the lush forest canopy', 'Annual Sioux River Folk Festival held in early August', 'Year-round recreation including cross-country skiing and snowshoeing', 'Historic site of Woodland Indian Culture burial mounds and artifacts']
attractions.tips: Visit during the fall (especially October) for the best hiking scenery and the annual FestiFall event. For birdwatchers, the Sergeant Creek area is highly recommended as it hosts over 200 species. Note that while motorized boats are allowed on Lake Lakota, it is a no-wake lake. Travel South Dakota, South Dakota Birding Hotspot

attractions.name: RedRossa Italian Grille
attractions.description: RedRossa Italian Grille is a full-service, slightly upscale restaurant that offers authentic Italian-American cuisine in a comfortable, festive atmosphere inspired by old-world Italy. The menu features an extensive selection of house-made pastas, wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas, hand-cut steaks, and flavorful seafood. It is known for its warm ambiance, including a large marble-topped bar and a Tuscan farmhouse-style interior.

attractions.name: Split Rock Creek State Park
attractions.description: Split Rock Creek State Park is a popular recreation destination featuring Split Rock Lake, the largest body of water in Pipestone County, created by a historic Sioux Quartzite dam in 1938. The park offers a scenic landscape of native prairie flowers and diverse wildlife, making it ideal for swimming, fishing, hiking, and camping. (Explore Minnesota, Outdoorsy)

attractions.hours: Monday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Tuesday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Wednesday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Thursday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Friday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Saturday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m., Sunday: 8 a.m. - 10 p.m. (Minnesota DNR)

highlights: ['Split Rock Lake (largest in Pipestone County)', 'Historic WPA Sioux Quartzite Dam and stone bridge', 'Scenic vistas from Prairie Hill', 'Sandy swimming beach', 'Canoe, kayak, and paddleboat rentals seasonal', '4.5 miles of hiking and horse trails']
attractions.tips: Stop by Prairie Hill for the best views of the landscape. Late summer is the ideal time to visit for vibrant prairie colors and wildflowers. All campsites are available by reservation only. Be sure to bring bug spray, as mosquitoes can be prevalent near the water. (MN Trail Fam, Outdoorsy)
